title: Dataset v4 large corpus for multi-day VM collection
description: Production-scale v4 corpus: control runs (false-positive anchor) plus heavy compact-a and compact-b repetition. Designed for unattended 1.5-2 day VM collection with retry-resilient export, per-run validation gates, and provenance recording. Total: 40 runs.
development_stage: v4 large for ML training and benchmark research

collection_strategy.why_batch_collection: Control runs first anchor the false-positive distribution. Compact-a and compact-b repeats give the ML training pool enough samples per scenario family to fit non-trivial models with run-level holdouts.

plans.purpose: Pure baseline runs (no faults). Anchor false-positive distribution and supply noise-class training data.
Latency, dependency outage, restart, Redis degradation, bad configuration, and near-miss coverage.
Service-diverse outages, frontend and Redis restarts, intermittent Redis failure, and noisy traffic coverage.

benchmark_tracks.description: Primary: classify each telemetry window as ticket_worthy / borderline / noise.
Triage classification grounded in time-ordered Jira memory retrieval.
Secondary: given a Jira issue, rank candidate telemetry episodes.

promotion_gates.raw_runs: Every collected run must pass validate-dataset-run.ps1 with 0 errors. Every run must pass validate-run-feature-distribution.ps1 with 0 hard failures.
promotion_gates.derived_outputs: Per-run triage_examples.jsonl must populate all FEATURE_COLUMNS with >=1 non-zero value across the run. Control runs are exempt from ticket_worthy + borderline coverage; fault runs must contain all three labels.
promotion_gates.aggregate_outputs: Global triage dataset must include all 13 scenario families with at least 30 windows per family.
